_comment: AI tool product data for AIfocus. Products support multi-category assignment via the 'categories' array. Scores use 3 dimensions (each 1-10): ease_of_use, academic_value, price_to_value. Overall is their simple average. affiliateSlug references data/affiliates.json entries.

products.name: ChatGPT Plus
products.freeTierNote: Free plan with GPT-3.5; Plus unlocks GPT-4
pros: Exceptional natural-language fluency across disciplines
Supports long-form academic drafts and outlines
Plugin ecosystem for citations and data analysis
Frequent model upgrades at no extra cost
cons: Can hallucinate references if not fact-checked
Limited real-time web access on free tier
products.verdict: The most versatile AI writing assistant for academics. GPT-4 quality justifies the subscription for researchers producing regular written output.

products.name: Consensus
products.freeTierNote: Free plan with 20 searches per month
pros: Searches across 200M+ peer-reviewed papers
AI-powered yes/no consensus indicators on research questions
Extracts key findings without reading full papers
Citation-ready output with DOI links
cons: No full-text PDF access — links to publisher
products.verdict: A game-changer for literature reviews. Consensus turns hours of database searching into minutes of evidence-based answers.

products.name: Midjourney
pros: Industry-leading image quality and coherence
Strong at scientific and technical illustration styles
Active community sharing prompts and techniques
Rapid iteration with variation and upscale tools
cons: Discord-only interface has a learning curve
Limited control over precise diagram layouts
products.verdict: The gold standard for AI image generation. Academics will find it invaluable for conference posters, presentation visuals, and conceptual illustrations.

products.name: GitHub Copilot
products.freeTierNote: Free for verified students and educators
products.useCase: Code completion, data scripts, analysis pipelines
pros: Inline code suggestions in VS Code, JetBrains, and Neovim
Excels at Python, R, and MATLAB — key academic languages
Free tier for students and educators via GitHub Education
Chat mode explains complex code and suggests refactors
products.verdict: Essential for academics who write code. The free student tier and deep editor integration make it the easiest productivity win in research computing.

products.name: Elicit
products.freeTierNote: Free plan with 5,000 credits per month
products.useCase: Systematic reviews, paper discovery, data extraction
pros: Purpose-built for academic research workflows
Extracts structured data from papers automatically
Identifies methodological details across studies
Generous free tier for individual researchers
cons: Smaller paper index than Google Scholar
Export options limited on free plan
products.verdict: The most research-aware AI tool available. Elicit understands academic papers at a structural level that general-purpose chatbots cannot match.

products.name: Grammarly with AI
products.freeTierNote: Free plan with basic grammar and spelling checks
pros: Works inline across browsers, Word, and Google Docs
Academic tone detector helps match journal conventions
AI rewrite suggestions preserve original meaning
Plagiarism checker included in premium plan
cons: AI generative features require Premium subscription
products.verdict: The most seamless writing enhancement tool. Its browser integration means every email, paper draft, and peer review gets automatic polish.

products.name: Claude Pro
products.freeTierNote: Free plan with limited daily messages; Pro unlocks extended usage and priority access
pros: Exceptional at nuanced academic writing with minimal hallucination
200K token context window handles entire papers and datasets
Strong reasoning for complex research questions and data analysis
Projects feature organises research context across conversations
cons: No native web browsing or real-time information access
Usage caps on Pro tier during peak demand periods
products.verdict: The most thoughtful AI assistant for academic work. Claude's combination of long context, careful reasoning, and low hallucination rate makes it the top choice for researchers who need reliability over flash.
